From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Lost British science fiction film by Bruce Gordon

| The First Men in the Moon | |
| --- | --- |
| The British film *The First Men in the Moon* (1919), adapted from [H. G. Wells](/wiki/H._G._Wells "H. G. Wells")' [novel of the same name](/wiki/The_First_Men_in_the_Moon "The First Men in the Moon"), was the first [science-fiction](/wiki/Science-fiction "Science-fiction") full length movie but is currently deemed [lost](/wiki/Lost_film "Lost film"). | |
| Directed by | [Bruce Gordon](/wiki/Bruce_Gordon_(actor/director) "Bruce Gordon (actor/director)") J. L. V. Leigh |
| Written by | R. Byron Webber  [H. G. Wells](/wiki/H._G._Wells "H. G. Wells") (novel) |
| Starring | [Bruce Gordon](/wiki/Bruce_Gordon_(actor/director) "Bruce Gordon (actor/director)") [Heather Thatcher](/wiki/Heather_Thatcher "Heather Thatcher") [Lionel d'Aragon](/wiki/Lionel_d%27Aragon "Lionel d'Aragon") |
| Distributed by | [Gaumont British](/wiki/Gaumont_British "Gaumont British") (UK) |
| Release date | * 1919 (1919) |
| Country | United Kingdom |
| Language | English intertitles |

***The First Men in the Moon*** is a 1919 black-and-white [silent film](/wiki/Silent_film "Silent film"), directed by [Bruce Gordon](/wiki/Bruce_Gordon_(actor/director) "Bruce Gordon (actor/director)") and J. L. V. Leigh. The film was based on [H. G. Wells](/wiki/H._G._Wells "H. G. Wells")' 1901 science fiction novel *[The First Men in the Moon](/wiki/The_First_Men_in_the_Moon "The First Men in the Moon")*.

As of August 2010, the film is not held in the [BFI National Archive](/wiki/BFI_National_Archive "BFI National Archive") and is listed on the [British Film Institute](/wiki/British_Film_Institute "British Film Institute")'s "[75 Most Wanted](/wiki/BFI_75_Most_Wanted "BFI 75 Most Wanted")" list of [lost films](/wiki/Lost_film "Lost film").[[1]](#cite_note-BFI-1) Stills from the production and a plot synopsis exist.[[2]](#cite_note-Godwin-2)

## Plot

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_First_Men_in_the_Moon_(1919_film)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Plot")]

The synopsis from *The Bioscope* trade paper of 5 June 1919 reads as follows:

> In the company of Rupert Bedford, a grasping speculator, Samson Cavor, an elderly inventor-scientist, ascends to the Moon in a sphere coated with 'Cavorite', a substance which has the property of neutralizing the law of gravity. After strange adventures with the 'Selenites' (the inhabitants of the Moon), Bedford villainously deserts the professor and returns to Earth alone in order to make a fortune for himself out of Cavorite. By means of wireless telegraphy, however, Hogben, a young engineer in love with Cavor's niece, Susan, succeeds in getting in touch with the stranded inventor, who denounces Bedford and states that he has been amicably received by the Grand Lunar, overlord of the Selenites. Susan thereupon indignantly rejects the proposals of Bedford, who has represented it as Cavor's last wish that she should marry him, and, instead, accepts Hogben as her husband.[[1]](#cite_note-BFI-1)

## Notability

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_First_Men_in_the_Moon_(1919_film)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Notability")]

[Robert Godwin](/wiki/Robert_Godwin "Robert Godwin") credits the film as "the first movie to ever be based entirely on a famous science fiction novel."[[2]](#cite_note-Godwin-2) *[Frankenstein](/wiki/Frankenstein_(1910_film) "Frankenstein (1910 film)")*, a loose adaptation of [Mary Shelley](/wiki/Mary_Shelley "Mary Shelley")'s 1818 [eponymous novel](/wiki/Frankenstein "Frankenstein"), however, had appeared in 1910, with a running time of 14 minutes, and Universal Pictures released a full-length feature film of [Jules Verne](/wiki/Jules_Verne "Jules Verne")'s 1870 novel *[Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Seas](/wiki/Twenty_Thousand_Leagues_Under_the_Seas "Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Seas")* in 1916.

## Cast

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_First_Men_in_the_Moon_(1919_film)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Cast")]

* [Bruce Gordon](/wiki/Bruce_Gordon_(actor/director) "Bruce Gordon (actor/director)") as Hogben
* [Heather Thatcher](/wiki/Heather_Thatcher "Heather Thatcher") as Susan
* [Hector Abbas](/wiki/Hector_Abbas "Hector Abbas") as Sampson Cavor
* [Lionel d'Aragon](/wiki/Lionel_d%27Aragon "Lionel d'Aragon") as Rupert Bedford
* [Cecil Morton York](/wiki/Cecil_Morton_York "Cecil Morton York") as Grand Lunar

## See also

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_First_Men_in_the_Moon_(1919_film)&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: See also")]

* [List of lost films](/wiki/List_of_lost_films "List of lost films")

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_First_Men_in_the_Moon_(1919_film)&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: References")]

London: [British Film Institute](/wiki/British_Film_Institute "British Film Institute"). 23 December 2010. Archived from [the original](http://old.bfi.org.uk/nationalarchive/news/mostwanted/first-men-in-the-moon.html) on 14 May 2019. Retrieved 23 June 2020 – via Internet Archive.
2. ^ [***a***](#cite_ref-Godwin_2-0) [***b***](#cite_ref-Godwin_2-1) Robert Godwin, *H.G. Wells The First Men in the Moon: the Story of the 1919 Film,* [Apogee Space Books](/wiki/Apogee_Space_Books "Apogee Space Books"), [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [978-1926837-31-4](/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-1926837-31-4 "Special:BookSources/978-1926837-31-4")- see [web page at Apogee books](http://www.apogeeprime.com/prime/bookpages/9781926837314.html) (retrieved 5 May 2014).
